metadata.name: Manage Image Mode-based Systems
metadata.description: Manage, inspect, modify, and update a server that uses image mode for Red Hat Enterprise Linux
objectives: Update an image-mode based server to a new image to add new tools and features
Revert the server to a previous image to undo the changes
prerequisites: Completion of the Install Red Hat Enterprise Linux by Using Image Mode exercise
Understanding of bootc commands for image management

tasks.name: Verify local index.html modification on serverc
tasks.description: The index.html file on serverc should contain local modifications
hints: Run: echo 'Local content edited on serverc.' >> /var/www/html/index.html
The /var directory is mutable in image mode

tasks.name: Verify updated Containerfile with additional packages
tasks.description: The Containerfile should include mod_ssl and vim-enhanced packages
tasks.success_message: Containerfile correctly updated with additional packages
tasks.failure_message: Containerfile missing mod_ssl and vim-enhanced installation
hints: Add: RUN dnf -y install mod_ssl vim-enhanced && dnf clean all
This adds SSL support and the vim editor to the image

tasks.name: Verify Containerfile creates new index.html content
tasks.description: The Containerfile should create modified index.html content

checks.pattern: Hello from a modified image-mode based installation

hints: Add a RUN directive to create new index.html content
The content should say 'Hello from a modified image-mode based installation!'

tasks.name: Verify updated image pushed to registry
tasks.description: The updated container image should be in the registry
tasks.failure_message: Updated container image not found in registry

tasks.name: Verify serverc rolled back to original image
tasks.description: After the exercise, serverc should be rolled back to the original image without mod_ssl
tasks.success_message: serverc successfully rolled back - packages not present
tasks.failure_message: serverc still has upgraded packages installed
hints: Verify: rpm -q mod_ssl vim-enhanced (should show not installed)

tasks.name: Verify HTTPS not available after rollback
tasks.description: After rollback, the HTTPS service should not be available
tasks.failure_message: HTTPS is still available - rollback may not be complete
hints: After rollback, mod_ssl is not installed so HTTPS should fail
Run: curl --insecure https://serverc.lab.example.com (should fail to connect)

tasks.name: Verify HTTP still works after rollback
tasks.description: The HTTP service should still work after rollback with local modifications preserved

checks.command: curl -s http://serverc.lab.example.com | grep -q 'Local content edited on serverc'

tasks.success_message: HTTP still working with local modifications preserved
tasks.failure_message: HTTP not working or local modifications lost
hints: Local modifications in /var are preserved across updates and rollbacks

tasks.name: Verify bootc status shows rollback state
tasks.description: The bootc status should show the current booted image after rollback
tasks.failure_message: bootc status not showing expected state
hints: Should show the original image as the Booted image
